revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Matsubayashiyama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsubayama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shōrinzan Kofun |image = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft}} |location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], Shingai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 10.48 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 53|coordinates = 16.70 |omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |AccompanyingTombs = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the [[:en:Ōta_River|Ōta River]] near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tōkaidō Shinkansen|ja|東海道新幹線|12=draft}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:松林山古墳]] [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Matsubayashiyama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsubayama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shōrinzan Kofun |image = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft}} |location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], Shingai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 10.48 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 53|coordinates = 16.70 |omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |subsidiary sites = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the [[:en:Ōta_River|Ōta River]] near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tōkaidō Shinkansen|ja|東海道新幹線|12=draft}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:松林山古墳]] [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:松林山古墳]] [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Matsubayashiyama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsubayama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shōrinzan Kofun |image = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], Shingai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 10.48 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 53|coordinates = 16.70 |omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |subsidiary sites = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the [[:en:Ōta_River|Ōta River]] near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tōkaidō Shinkansen|ja|東海道新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft|12=draft}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]]

It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Matsubayashiyama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsubayama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shōrinzan Kofun |image = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], Shingai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 10.48 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 53|coordinates = 16.70 |omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |subsidiary sites = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the [[:en:Ōta_River|Ōta River]] near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tōkaidō Shinkansen|ja|東海道新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft|12=draft}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]]

Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Matsubayashiyama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsubayama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shōrinzan Kofun |image = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], Shingai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 10.48 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 53|coordinates = 16.70 |omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |subsidiary sites = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the [[:en:Ōta_River|Ōta River]] near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tōkaidō Shinkansen|ja|東海道新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft|12=draft}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]]

Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations.
