revision.comment: adding secondary category for the ones still with unknown parameters
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Matsubayashiyama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsubayama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shōrinzan Kofun |image = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], Shingai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 10.48 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 53|coordinates = 16.70 |omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |subsidiary sites = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the [[:en:Ōta_River|Ōta River]] near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tōkaidō Shinkansen|ja|東海道新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft|12=draft}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]]

Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Matsubayashiyama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsubayama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shōrinzan Kofun |image = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], Shingai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 10.48 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 53|coordinates = 16.70 |omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |subsidiary sites = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the [[:en:Ōta_River|Ōta River]] near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tōkaidō Shinkansen|ja|東海道新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft|12=draft}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]]

Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], Shingai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 10.48 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 53|coordinates = 16.70 |omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |subsidiary sites = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the [[:en:Ōta_River|Ōta River]] near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tōkaidō Shinkansen|ja|東海道新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft|12=draft}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|12=draft" to ""
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Matsubayashiyama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳}} {{ill|Matsubayama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shōrinzan Kofun |image = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|ja|御厨古墳群}} |location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], Shingai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 10.48 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 53|coordinates = 16.70 |omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |subsidiary sites = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the [[:en:Ōta_River|Ōta River]] near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tōkaidō Shinkansen|ja|東海道新幹線}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]]

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:松林山古墳 up to rev 99995288
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 松林山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[御厨古墳群]] |所在地 =[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]新貝 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 43|緯度秒 = 10.48 |経度度 = 137|経度分 = 53|経度秒 = 16.70 |ISO = JP-22 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長107m |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]] |出土品 = 銅鏡ほか副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「[[御厨古墳群]]」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 |ラベル = 松林山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''松林山古墳'''（しょうりんざんこふん）は、[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]新貝にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[御厨古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 == 静岡県西部、[[天竜川]]東岸の磐田原台地南東縁、[[太田川 (静岡県)|太田川]]が形成する沖積平野を望む位置に築造された大型前方後円墳である。墳丘のうち後円部は良好に遺存するが、前方部ではかつて茶畑が営まれたほか[[東海道新幹線]]の工事の際に一部が削平を受けている。[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）に埋葬施設の発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘は後円部では3段築成。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形・壺形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（盾形・家形埴輪か）が検出されている{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]で、石室主軸を墳丘主軸と直交する南北方向とする{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。石室全長7.9メートルを測る長大な石室であり、板石の小口積みによって構築される{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。未盗掘の状態で発掘調査がなされており、[[内行花文鏡]]・[[三角縁神獣鏡]]などの銅鏡4面のほか、琴柱形石製品・石釧・貝釧などの装身具類、武器・武具類など多数の副葬品が検出されている{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。特に珍しい遺物としては[[スイジガイ]]製の貝釧がある{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|御厨古墳群パンフレット|2009}}。静岡県ひいては東海地方における代表的な前期古墳であるとともに、未盗掘状態で発掘調査がなされたことによって前期大型古墳の副葬品の組み合わせが明らかとなった点で重要視される古墳である{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|3294|御厨古墳群}}。なお、御厨古墳群では同時期の古墳として秋葉山古墳・稲荷山古墳があるが、松林山古墳は中央とのつながりが強い首長墓であり、秋葉山古墳・稲荷山古墳は松林山古墳とは別の在地首長墓と想定される。松林山古墳の後代首長墓としては南の高根山古墳が想定され、松林山古墳と同様に中央とのつながりが強い古墳になる{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf 新いわた文化財だより 第6号]}}（磐田市教育委員会文化財課、2005年（リンクは磐田市ホームページ））。。 古墳域は[[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）、主体部の発掘調査（[[後藤守一]]ら、[[1939年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 * [[1954年]]（昭和29年）、静岡県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年）、[[東海道新幹線]]建設に伴う発掘調査（静岡県教育委員会、[[1965年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、農道拡張に伴う発掘調査（磐田市埋蔵文化財センター、[[1992年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 * [[2001年]]（平成13年）3月26日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「御厨古墳群」のうち）{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|3294|御厨古墳群}}。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}史跡説明板。。 * 墳丘長：107メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：66.5メートル ** 高さ：10.6メートル File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む
{{small|前方部前方を[[東海道新幹線]]が通る。}} == 出土品 == [[ファイル:松林山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] [[ファイル:松林山古墳出土 貝釧.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|貝釧}}]] 1931年（昭和6年）の調査で竪穴式石室から出土した副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|松林山古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 * 銅鏡 4 ** [[内行花文鏡]] ** 仿製内行花文鏡 1 ** [[三角縁神獣鏡]] 1 ** 仿製四獣鏡 1 * 装身具類 ** 碧玉製琴柱形石製品 1 ** 碧玉製石釧 2 ** スイジガイ製釧 3 ** 硬玉製勾玉 ** 碧玉製管玉 * 武器・武具類 ** 大刀 ** 剣 ** 鉾 ** 鉄鏃 ** 銅鏃 ** 長方板革綴短甲 1 * 巴形銅器 3 * 農工具類 ** 鉇 ** 鉄斧 ** 刀子 ** 鋸 ** 鏨 ** 鎌 ** 砥石 これらの副葬品は、現在では[[東京国立博物館]]（[[東京都]][[台東区]]）で保管されている。近年の研究では、松林山古墳出土と大墓古墳（奈良県五條市）とで出土短甲の混同が判明しており、中期古墳の始まりの様相を再検討する必要が指摘される[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 中期古墳のイメージ]（東京国立博物館）。。 そのほかの出土品としては、前方部出土と伝える仿製鏡1面がある。また墳丘の発掘調査では、[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形・壺形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（盾形・家形埴輪か）が検出されている{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。このうち壺形埴輪には巴形の透かし孔が認められる{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 ファイル:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|内行花文鏡 ファイル:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|変形内行花文鏡 ファイル:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡 ファイル:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|四獣鏡 ファイル:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|長方板革綴短甲片 ファイル:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|方形板革綴短甲片
{{small|大墓古墳（奈良県五條市）出土品とされてきたが、近年に松林山古墳出土品と判明。}} == 関連施設 == * [[東京国立博物館]]（[[東京都]][[台東区]]） - 松林山古墳出土品等を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（磐田市教育委員会文化財課、2017年設置） * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf 「国史跡御厨古墳群」]}}（磐田市教育委員会文化財課、2009年）。|ref={{Harvid|御厨古墳群パンフレット|2009}}}} - リンクは磐田市立図書館「いわたデジタルアーカイブ」。 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2009|chapter=御厨古墳群|title=磐田の文化財|publisher=磐田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|磐田の文化財|2009}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=斎藤忠|authorlink=斎藤忠|chapter=松林山古墳|year=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|松林山古墳（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=向坂鋼二|chapter=松林山古墳|year=|title=[[世界大百科事典]]|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|松林山古墳（世界大百科）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=柴田稔|year=|chapter=松林山古墳|title=[[日本大百科全書]]（ニッポニカ）|publisher=[[小学館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|松林山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=松林山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|松林山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=松林山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * 報告書 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1939|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12564 静岡県磐田郡松林山古墳発掘調査報告]|publisher=静岡県磐田郡御厨村郷土教育研究会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 *** 復刻：{{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1975|chapter=|title=静岡県磐田郡松林山古墳発掘調査報告|publisher=静岡県文化財保存協会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1963|chapter=|title=静岡県文化財調査報告書 第2集|publisher=静岡県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=静岡県文化財調査報告書 第6集 -東海道新幹線静岡県内工事に伴う埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書-|publisher=静岡県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=東海道幹線増設工事に伴う埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書|publisher=日本国有鉄道|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=磐田市埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=松林山古墳発掘調査報告書 平成元年度 -市道鎌田袋井線拡幅に伴う発掘調査-|publisher=磐田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本達也|year=2005|chapter=古墳時代中期甲冑の出現と中期開始論 -松林山古墳と津堂城山古墳から-|title=待兼山考古学論集 -都出比呂志先生退任記念-|publisher=大阪大学考古学研究室|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[御厨古墳群]] * [[銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|3294|御厨古墳群}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しようりんさんこふん}} [[Category:静岡県の古墳]] [[Category:磐田市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:静岡県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[松林山古墳]] to [[Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 松林山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[御厨古墳群]] |所在地 =[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]新貝 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 43|緯度秒 = 10.48 |経度度 = 137|経度分 = 53|経度秒 = 16.70 |ISO = JP-22 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長107m |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]] |出土品 = 銅鏡ほか副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「[[御厨古墳群]]」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 |ラベル = 松林山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''松林山古墳'''（しょうりんざんこふん）は、[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]新貝にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[御厨古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 == 静岡県西部、[[天竜川]]東岸の磐田原台地南東縁、[[太田川 (静岡県)|太田川]]が形成する沖積平野を望む位置に築造された大型前方後円墳である。墳丘のうち後円部は良好に遺存するが、前方部ではかつて茶畑が営まれたほか[[東海道新幹線]]の工事の際に一部が削平を受けている。[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）に埋葬施設の発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘は後円部では3段築成。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形・壺形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（盾形・家形埴輪か）が検出されている{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]で、石室主軸を墳丘主軸と直交する南北方向とする{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。石室全長7.9メートルを測る長大な石室であり、板石の小口積みによって構築される{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。未盗掘の状態で発掘調査がなされており、[[内行花文鏡]]・[[三角縁神獣鏡]]などの銅鏡4面のほか、琴柱形石製品・石釧・貝釧などの装身具類、武器・武具類など多数の副葬品が検出されている{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。特に珍しい遺物としては[[スイジガイ]]製の貝釧がある{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|御厨古墳群パンフレット|2009}}。静岡県ひいては東海地方における代表的な前期古墳であるとともに、未盗掘状態で発掘調査がなされたことによって前期大型古墳の副葬品の組み合わせが明らかとなった点で重要視される古墳である{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|3294|御厨古墳群}}。なお、御厨古墳群では同時期の古墳として秋葉山古墳・稲荷山古墳があるが、松林山古墳は中央とのつながりが強い首長墓であり、秋葉山古墳・稲荷山古墳は松林山古墳とは別の在地首長墓と想定される。松林山古墳の後代首長墓としては南の高根山古墳が想定され、松林山古墳と同様に中央とのつながりが強い古墳になる{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf 新いわた文化財だより 第6号]}}（磐田市教育委員会文化財課、2005年（リンクは磐田市ホームページ））。。 古墳域は[[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）、主体部の発掘調査（[[後藤守一]]ら、[[1939年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 * [[1954年]]（昭和29年）、静岡県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年）、[[東海道新幹線]]建設に伴う発掘調査（静岡県教育委員会、[[1965年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、農道拡張に伴う発掘調査（磐田市埋蔵文化財センター、[[1992年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 * [[2001年]]（平成13年）3月26日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「御厨古墳群」のうち）{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|3294|御厨古墳群}}。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}史跡説明板。。 * 墳丘長：107メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：66.5メートル ** 高さ：10.6メートル File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む
{{small|前方部前方を[[東海道新幹線]]が通る。}} == 出土品 == [[ファイル:松林山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] [[ファイル:松林山古墳出土 貝釧.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|貝釧}}]] 1931年（昭和6年）の調査で竪穴式石室から出土した副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|松林山古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 * 銅鏡 4 ** [[内行花文鏡]] ** 仿製内行花文鏡 1 ** [[三角縁神獣鏡]] 1 ** 仿製四獣鏡 1 * 装身具類 ** 碧玉製琴柱形石製品 1 ** 碧玉製石釧 2 ** スイジガイ製釧 3 ** 硬玉製勾玉 ** 碧玉製管玉 * 武器・武具類 ** 大刀 ** 剣 ** 鉾 ** 鉄鏃 ** 銅鏃 ** 長方板革綴短甲 1 * 巴形銅器 3 * 農工具類 ** 鉇 ** 鉄斧 ** 刀子 ** 鋸 ** 鏨 ** 鎌 ** 砥石 これらの副葬品は、現在では[[東京国立博物館]]（[[東京都]][[台東区]]）で保管されている。近年の研究では、松林山古墳出土と大墓古墳（奈良県五條市）とで出土短甲の混同が判明しており、中期古墳の始まりの様相を再検討する必要が指摘される[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 中期古墳のイメージ]（東京国立博物館）。。 そのほかの出土品としては、前方部出土と伝える仿製鏡1面がある。また墳丘の発掘調査では、[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形・壺形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（盾形・家形埴輪か）が検出されている{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。このうち壺形埴輪には巴形の透かし孔が認められる{{Sfn|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 ファイル:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|内行花文鏡 ファイル:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|変形内行花文鏡 ファイル:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡 ファイル:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|四獣鏡 ファイル:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|長方板革綴短甲片 ファイル:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|方形板革綴短甲片
{{small|大墓古墳（奈良県五條市）出土品とされてきたが、近年に松林山古墳出土品と判明。}} == 関連施設 == * [[東京国立博物館]]（[[東京都]][[台東区]]） - 松林山古墳出土品等を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（磐田市教育委員会文化財課、2017年設置） * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf 「国史跡御厨古墳群」]}}（磐田市教育委員会文化財課、2009年）。|ref={{Harvid|御厨古墳群パンフレット|2009}}}} - リンクは磐田市立図書館「いわたデジタルアーカイブ」。 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2009|chapter=御厨古墳群|title=磐田の文化財|publisher=磐田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|磐田の文化財|2009}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=斎藤忠|authorlink=斎藤忠|chapter=松林山古墳|year=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|松林山古墳（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=向坂鋼二|chapter=松林山古墳|year=|title=[[世界大百科事典]]|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|松林山古墳（世界大百科）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=柴田稔|year=|chapter=松林山古墳|title=[[日本大百科全書]]（ニッポニカ）|publisher=[[小学館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|松林山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=松林山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|松林山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=松林山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|松林山古墳（平凡社）|2000}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * 報告書 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1939|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12564 静岡県磐田郡松林山古墳発掘調査報告]|publisher=静岡県磐田郡御厨村郷土教育研究会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 *** 復刻：{{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1975|chapter=|title=静岡県磐田郡松林山古墳発掘調査報告|publisher=静岡県文化財保存協会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1963|chapter=|title=静岡県文化財調査報告書 第2集|publisher=静岡県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=静岡県文化財調査報告書 第6集 -東海道新幹線静岡県内工事に伴う埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書-|publisher=静岡県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=東海道幹線増設工事に伴う埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書|publisher=日本国有鉄道|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=磐田市埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=松林山古墳発掘調査報告書 平成元年度 -市道鎌田袋井線拡幅に伴う発掘調査-|publisher=磐田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本達也|year=2005|chapter=古墳時代中期甲冑の出現と中期開始論 -松林山古墳と津堂城山古墳から-|title=待兼山考古学論集 -都出比呂志先生退任記念-|publisher=大阪大学考古学研究室|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[御厨古墳群]] * [[銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|3294|御厨古墳群}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しようりんさんこふん}} [[Category:静岡県の古墳]] [[Category:磐田市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:静岡県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Matsubayashiyama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳}} {{ill|Matsubayama Kofun|ja|松林山古墳}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shōrinzan Kofun |image = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|ja|御厨古墳群}} |location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], Shingai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 10.48 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 53|coordinates = 16.70 |omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |subsidiary sites = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the [[:en:Ōta_River|Ōta River]] near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tōkaidō Shinkansen|ja|東海道新幹線}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]] {{translated page|ja|松林山古墳|version=99995288|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft}} |location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], Shingai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 10.48 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 53|coordinates = 16.70 |omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |subsidiary sites = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the [[:en:Ōta_River|Ōta River]] near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tōkaidō Shinkansen|ja|東海道新幹線|12=draft}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * **1931 (Shōwa 6)**: Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1954 (Shōwa 29)**: Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **1961 (Shōwa 36)**: Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **1989 (Heisei 1)**: Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * **2001 (Heisei 13)**: Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * **Mound Length**: 107 meters * **Rear Circular Section**: Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * **Bronze Mirrors**: 4 ** {{ill|Flower mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft}} ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * **Jewelry and Ornaments**: ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=draft}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|ja|スイジガイ|12=draft}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * **Weapons and Armor**: ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * **Agricultural and Craft Tools**: ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|ja|御厨古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shōrinzan Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|松林山古墳|version=99995288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic sites in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Matsubayashiyama Kofun]] [[ja:松林山古墳]]
