revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Shorinzan Kofun]][[ja:松林山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Shorinzan Kofun]][[ja:松林山古墳]] [[ja:松林山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpōkōenfun]] |name = Shōrinzan Kofun |image = [[File:Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Mikuriya Kofun Group|WD=Q87769616|1=Mikuriyama Kofun group}} |location = {{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.71958333333333|N|137.88797222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-22 |type = {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area = Mound length: 107 m |burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical shaft-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |subsidiary sites = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} |InterredIndividual = |Mausoleum = |HistoricalSite = Included in the National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Mikuriya Kofun Group" |CulturalAssets = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Shizuoka |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-WSW |map_dot_label = Shōrinzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Shōrinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, in the Shingai area. It is a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|en|Mikuriyama Kofun group|WD=Q87769616|lt=Mikuriyama Kofun group|1=Mikuriyama Kofun group}}, a National Historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb is located on the southeastern edge of the Iwatahara Plateau, overlooking the alluvial plain typeed by the {{ill|Ōta_River|en|Ōta_River|WD=Q386095|lt=Ōta River|1=Ōta River}} near the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River|1=Tenryū River}} in western Shizuoka Prefecture. The rear circular section is well-preserved, while the front section has been partially damaged due to tea cultivation and construction work for the {{ill|Tokaido Shinkansen|en|Tokaido Shinkansen|WD=Q660895|lt=Tōkaidō Shinkansen|1=Tokaido Shinkansen}}. The burial facility was excavated in 1931 (Shōwa 6){{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented west. The rear circular section features a three-tier construction. Surface finds include paving stones, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning glory and jar-shaped variants), and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}} (shield-shaped and house-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a {{ill|vertical shaft-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented north-south, perpendicular to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The chamber is 7.9 meters long, constructed using tightly-fitted stone slabs. The tomb was excavated in an unlooted state, yielding four bronze mirrors (including a [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]] and a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}), stone burial goods such as bracelets and ornaments, and numerous other grave goods{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Mikuriya Kofun Group Pamphlet|2009}}. It is a representative early-period tomb in Shizuoka and the Tokai region, notable for its rich and intact assemblage of grave goods, which illuminate the burial practices of early large-map_size tombs{{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}}. Within the Mikuriya Kofun Group, contemporary tombs such as the Akibayama and Inariyama kofun are considered local chief tombs, while Shōrinzan is thought to be a chief tomb connected to central authorities{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/007/901/0509.pdf Iwata Heritage Newsletter Issue 6]}} (Iwata City Board of Education, 2005).. The tomb area was Historic site a National Historic site in 2001 (Heisei 13). == Archaeological History == * '''1931 (Shōwa 6)''': Excavation of the burial chamber by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|1=Shūichi Gotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488456}} and others; report published in 1939{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * '''1954 (Shōwa 29)''': Designated a Historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture. * '''1961 (Shōwa 36)''': Excavation during Tōkaidō Shinkansen construction; report published in 1965 by the Shizuoka Prefecture Board of Education{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * '''1989 (Heisei 1)''': Excavation due to agricultural road expansion; report published in 1992 by the Iwata City Archaeological Center{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. * '''2001 (Heisei 13)''': Designated a National Historic site as part of the Mikuriya Kofun Group. == Mound area == The mound area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division in 2017.: * '''Mound Length''': 107 meters * '''Rear Circular Section''': Three tiers ** Diameter: 66.5 meters ** Height: 10.6 meters File:Shorinzan Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Shorinzan Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front
{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * '''Bronze Mirrors: 4''' ** [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]] ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * '''Jewelry and Ornaments:''' ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|en|Harpago chiragra|WD=Q1257283|lt=Harpago chiragra|1=Harpago chiragra}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * '''Weapons and Armor:''' ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * '''Agricultural and Craft Tools:''' ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} in Tokyo. Recent research identified a mix-up between armor pieces attributed to Shōrinzan Kofun and those from Ōhako Kofun in Nara Prefecture, suggesting a need to reassess the early Kofun period's characteristics[https://www.tnm.jp/modules/r_exhibition/index.php?controller=item&id=6686 Kofun built Mid-built image] (Tokyo National Museum).. Other findings include figurative and cylindrical haniwa, with some jar-shaped haniwa exhibiting bar-shaped perforations. File:松林山古墳出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 変形内行花文鏡.JPG|Variant Naikō-kamonkyō File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁吾作銘二神二獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen mirror with inscription File:松林山古墳出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror File:松林山古墳出土 長方板革綴短甲片.JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment File:大墓古墳出土 方形板革綴短甲片 (J-9791).JPG|Rectangular plate armor fragment, previously attributed to Ōhako Kofun == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses Shōrinzan Kofun burial goods. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Sources used for article composition)}} * Explanatory board, installed by the Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2017. * {{PDFlink|[https://www.lib-iwata-shizuoka.jp/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/bmikuriya.pdf "National Historic site Mikuriya Kofun Group"]}} (Iwata City Board of Education Historic site Division, 2009). == See Also == * {{ill|Mikuriyama Kofun group|en|Mikuriyama Kofun group|WD=Q87769616|lt=Mikuriyama Kofun group|1=Mikuriyama Kofun group}} * {{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11458088|lt=Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shorinzan Kofun}} * {{Historic site Online Database|401|3294|Mikuriya Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|松林山古墳|version=99995288|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松林山古墳]] {{Commons category|Shorinzan Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q87853315}} [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Kofun of Shizuoka prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Shizuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpōkōenfun]]

{{small|The Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs in front of the mound.}} == burial goods == The burial chamber yielded numerous grave goods during the 1931 excavation{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shōrinzan Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}: * '''Bronze Mirrors: 4''' ** [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]] ** Variant Naikō-kamonkyō ** {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} ** Variant Four-Beast Mirror * '''Jewelry and Ornaments:''' ** Jasper {{ill|koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja|琴柱形石製品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koto pillar-shaped stone products|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Jasper stone bracelets (2) ** Spindle-shaped bracelets (3) made from {{ill|Harpago chiragra|en|Harpago chiragra|WD=Q1257283|lt=Harpago chiragra|1=Harpago chiragra}} ** Jadeite magatama ** Jasper tubular beads * '''Weapons and Armor:''' ** Swords ** Spears ** Arrowheads (iron and bronze) ** Rectangular plate armor * '''Agricultural and Craft Tools:''' ** Hoes ** Axes ** Small knives ** Saws ** Chisels ** Sickles ** Whetstones These burial goods are housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} in Tokyo.
